caveats: The user may have run verification outside indexed agent sessions.

open_questions: What is the smallest relevant command or browser task for this diff?

evidence_candidates.why_it_matters: A review without fresh verification evidence can only prove static plausibility, not that the change works.

evidence_procedure_steps.gate: No candidate is marked confirmed or fixed using stale or missing verification.

findings.suggestion: Return a Result/Option and reject insufficient funds: `self.balance = self.balance.checked_sub(amount).ok_or(InsufficientFunds)?;` (or saturating_sub if clamping to zero is the intended contract). Never use bare `-=` on a balance.
findings.summary: source.rs:17 `self.balance -= amount` with amount > balance underflows u64. Debug builds panic (process crash mid-transaction); release builds wrap to ~u64::MAX, so an over-withdrawal silently succeeds and inflates the account balance — money creation / data-integrity corruption on a public money type.
findings.title: debit() underflow: over-debit panics (debug) or wraps to a huge balance (release)

findings.suggestion: Use checked_add and surface the error: `self.balance = self.balance.checked_add(amount).ok_or(Overflow)?;` (or saturating_add if a ceiling is acceptable).
findings.summary: source.rs:11 `self.balance += amount` overflows u64 when balance+amount exceeds u64::MAX. Debug builds panic; release builds wrap, silently rolling a large balance back down to a tiny value — the user's funds effectively disappear. Public API on a monetary type.
findings.title: credit() overflow: large credit panics (debug) or wraps balance to a small value (release)

findings.suggestion: Apply the prescribed checked arithmetic, then add unit tests for the overflow/underflow edges (e.g. credit(u64::MAX)+1, debit(amount > balance)) and provide a minimal crate (Cargo.toml + lib.rs) so `cargo test` can be run. Next agent must attach that fresh test output before marking fixed.
findings.summary: Both methods carry `// BUG:` comments (lines 8-10, 15-16) that prescribe checked_add/saturating_add, yet the raw `+=`/`-=` remain (lines 11, 17) — an incomplete handoff / fake-green risk. There is no Cargo.toml, no #[test], and no caller (confirmed by find/grep), so the rerun_relevant_verification gate is unsatisfiable as shipped: nothing proves these paths work.
findings.title: Fix identified in comments but never applied; no tests or verification

nodes.label: rerun_relevant_verification

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

New file implements an Account ledger with unchecked u64 arithmetic in both credit() and debit(). Both operations carry unenforced invariants (no-overflow on credit, sufficient-funds on debit) that Rust does not guard in release builds, so over-debit inflates balances and over-credit corrupts them. The code comments accurately describe the bugs and the correct fix, so they will not misdirect the next agent — but the guarded implementation they prescribe is missing. debit() is the higher-severity of the two (silent balance/money creation).

New public monetary type Account uses unchecked `+=`/`-=` on u64 balances. Both credit (line 11) and debit (line 17) are real correctness/safety bugs: debug builds panic on over/underflow (crash), release builds wrap silently — the debit underflow is the most dangerous, letting an over-debit produce a near-u64::MAX balance (money creation) on a financial type. Currently 0 callers so present blast radius is low, but these are pub methods that are wrong the moment they're used. Fix with checked_/saturating_ arithmetic.

New Account primitive with two genuine correctness/security bugs in credit (line 11) and debit (line 17): unchecked u64 arithmetic that panics in debug and wraps in release. debit underflow is the worst — over-debit wraps to a huge balance (money creation) and the u64 return type gives callers no way to detect failure. Both bugs are documented in BUG comments but the prescribed fix was never applied, and there are no tests, no crate, and no callers, so nothing has been verified. Blast radius is currently 0 callers (newly added), but the primitive is unsafe by contract for any future caller.
